DISCOVERY, 302, PLAINTIFF ADELAIDA GONGORA MTN TO COMPEL RESPONSES IN SUPP OF MONETARY SANCTION REQ (FOR TENTATIVE RULING ONLY) OFF CALENDAR (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ADELAIDA GONGORA'S MOTION FOR ORDER THAT MATTERS IN REQUUEST FOR ADMISSIONS BE DEEMED ADMITTED IS GRANTED. THE COURT CONSIDERED DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION, WHICH WAS FILED TWO DAYS LATE, AND THE SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF MATTHEW J. WEBB, WHICH WAS FILED ON JULY 29, 2014. DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION FAILS TO SHOW THAT VERIFIED RESPONSES WERE IN FACT SERVED ON THE PLAINTIFF. THE EXHIBITS ONLY PROVIDED THE FIRST PAGE OF THE RESPONSES AND THE PROOF OF SERVICE. THE SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF MATTHEW J. WEBB SHOWS THAT THE RESPONSES SERVED ON PLAINTIFF COUNSEL ON JUNE 30, 2014 WERE UNVERIFIED. "AN UNVERIFIED RESPONSE IS INEFFECTIVE; IT IS THE EQUIVALENT OF NO RESPONSES AT ALL." WEIL AND BROWN, CAL PRAC. GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (TRG 2014) 8:1364.1 CITING APPLETON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1988) 206 CA3D 632, 636. THE DECLARATION OF MATTHEW J. WEBB IS NOT PERSUASIVE. THE TRIAL DATE IS AUGUST 18, 2014. NO MOTION HAS BEEN FILED TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL. THE DISCOVERY WAS SERVED ON MAY 2, 2014. ACCORDING TO DEFENSE COUNSEL'S DECLARATION, HE DID NOT LEARN THAT DEFENDANT NADEEM MOHAMMAD WAS OUT OF THE COUNTRY UNTIL THREE WEEKS AFTER THE VERIFIED RESPONSES WERE DUE. FURTHERMORE, COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS DID NOT SEEK AN ORDER TO EXTEND THE TIME TO PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS NADEEM MOHAMMAD AND SHABBIR MUGHAL IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 2033.280(C). SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $700.00 ARE TO BE MADE PAYABLE TO ADELAIDA GONGORA AND HER ATTORNEYS OF RECORD, LIBERATION LAW GROUP NO LATER THAN AUGUST 18, 2014. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

Mtn To Compel Responses In Supp Of Monetary Sanction Req
Off Calendar Aug-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. UN KEI WU OF THE LIBERATION LAW GROUP, P.C., TEL. NO. 695-1000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ADELAIDA GONGORA's Motion To Compel Responses In Support Of Monetary Sanction Request - Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to: (1) Form Interrogatories, Set #1-General; (2) Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set #1, Special Interrogatories Set, #1; and Request for Production of Documents, Set #1 on Nadeem Mohammad dba City Pizza and Yucatecan Food and Shabbir Mughal are granted. The Court considered Defendants' Opposition, which was filed two days late, and the Supplemental Declaration of Matthew J. Webb, which was filed on July 29, 2014. Defendants' opposition fails to show that verified responses were in fact served on the plaintiff as the exhibits only provided the first page of the responses and the proof of service. The Supplemental Declaration of Matthew J. Webb shows that the responses served on Plaintiff counsel on June 30, 2014 were unverified. "An unverified response is ineffective; it is the equivalent of no responses at all" Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2014) 8:1113 citing Appleton v. Superior Court (1988) 206 CA3d 632, 636. The Declaration of Matthew J. Webb is not persuasive. The trial date is August 18, 2014. Defendants and their counsel were aware of the trial date since they were served with the trial date notice by the court on January 8, 2014. No motion has been filed to continue the trial.. Furthermore, the discovery was served on May 2, 2014. According to defense counsel's declaration, he did not learn that defendant Nadeem Mohammad was out of the country until three weeks after the verified responses were due. Furthermore, counsel for defendants did not seek an order to extend the time to provide verified responses. Defendants Nadeem Mohammad dba City Pizza and Yucatecan Food and Shabbir Mughal to serve verified responses without objection and produce documents responsive to the discovery no later than August 15, 2014. Service shall be made by hand delivery or overnight service. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendants Nadeem Mohammad and Shabbir Mughal is granted. Sanctions in the amount of $2,000 are to be made payable to Adelaida Gongora and her attorneys of record, Liberation Law Group no later than August 18, 2014. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM

Mtn For Order That Matters In Req For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Aug-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. UN KEI WU OF THE LIBERATION LAW GROUP, P.C., TEL. NO. 695-1000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ADELAIDA GONGORA's Motion For Order That Matters In Requuest For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted is Granted. The Court considered Defendants' Opposition, which was filed two days late, and the Supplemental Declaration of Matthew J. Webb, which was filed on July 29, 2014. Defendants' opposition fails to show that verified responses were in fact served on the plaintiff. The exhibits only provided the first page of the responses and the proof of service. The Supplemental Declaration of Matthew J. Webb shows that the responses served on Plaintiff counsel on June 30, 2014 were unverified. "An unverified response is ineffective; it is the equivalent of no responses at all." Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2014) 8:1364.1 citing Appleton v. Superior Court (1988) 206 CA3d 632, 636. The Declaration of Matthew J. Webb is not persuasive. The trial date is August 18, 2014. No motion has been filed to continue the trial. The discovery was served on May 2, 2014. According to defense counsel's declaration, he did not learn that defendant Nadeem Mohammad was out of the country until three weeks after the verified responses were due. Furthermore, counsel for defendants did not seek an order to extend the time to provide verified responses. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendants Nadeem Mohammad and Shabbir Mughal is granted pursuant to CCP 2033.280(c). Sanctions in the amount of $700.00 are to be made payable to Adelaida Gongora and her attorneys of record, Liberation Law Group no later than August 18, 2014.
ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm
